title: support for macvlan & ipvlan added.
body:
CNI plugins for macvlan and ipvlan will be invoked
instead treating them as user defined network

Signed-off-by: Kunal Kushwaha <kushwaha_kunal_v7@lab.ntt.co.jp>

comments.body:
Currently, if `ipvlan` or `macvlan` is provided as option for container network, it treats as user defined network and skip the network creation using CNI plugin.

Since with CNI default setup, both `macvlan` and `ipvlan` is provided, support for both will be good addition.

e.g. 
```
podman  run -it --rm --network=macvlan busybox sh
```

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *giuseppe*, *kunalkushwaha* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Can we please do this without overloading `--net` anymore? That flag has gotten ridiculous to parse
Also - does this actually hit CNI at any point? I don't know if specifying `macvlan` actually does anything without a CNI network explicitly named "macvlan"

This issues is fixed with https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/2796.

> Also - does this actually hit CNI at any point? I don't know if specifying macvlan actually does anything without a CNI network explicitly named "macvlan

@mheon Thanks for pointing it out. I realized now, it was being  created as `bridge` network, even `macvlan` or `ipvlan` was passed.

Closing this PR as its  fixed with https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/2796.
